headline: MIXED SIGNALS AHEAD OF CRITICAL PLANTING WEEK
subheadline: Grains steady, cattle strong, energy weak as weather takes command.
lead: Commodity markets enter a pivotal week with mixed signals across the board. Corn held flat at $4.63 as of Friday's close while December contracts edged higher to $4.84, suggesting cautious optimism despite planting pressure. With optimal planting windows narrowing and weather forecasts becoming the primary driver, producers face tough decisions on pricing and risk management.
teaser: Weather takes the wheel as planting deadlines loom and markets show divergent signals.

one_number.context: The 1.5% Friday drop pulled hogs back below the psychologically important $102 level. With pork demand softening seasonally and production ramping up, this breakdown suggests the spring rally may be stalling. Hog producers should watch whether Monday's open holds this level.

sections.title: Week Ahead Setup
sections.body: Monday morning will test whether Friday's mixed close was positioning or genuine direction. December corn at $4.84 sits in a critical range where weather premium could either build or evaporate quickly. Soybeans closed stronger at $11.79, but the real test comes with this week's planting progress reports. Energy weakness (crude down 2.3% to $94.40) could provide input cost relief if it holds.
sections.bottom_line: Weather forecasts will drive more price action than any fundamental report this week.

sections.title: Cattle Momentum Continues
sections.body: Both live and feeder cattle posted solid 0.7% gains Friday, with live cattle at $245.23 maintaining their position in the upper 80% of the 52-week range. Strong cash basis and tight supplies continue supporting the complex. Feeders at $361.77 reflect confidence in placement demand despite higher corn prices. The momentum looks sustainable given current supply fundamentals.
sections.bottom_line: Cattle strength has legs; tight supplies override feed cost concerns.
sections.farmer_action: Price remaining fed cattle if live futures hold above $245 through Wednesday's close.

sections.title: Energy Divergence
sections.body: Crude's 2.3% drop to $94.40 Friday signals potential relief on diesel costs, but natural gas at $2.68 remains stubbornly near 52-week lows. This creates an unusual dynamic where propane costs could stay manageable while diesel volatility persists. The energy complex lacks clear direction heading into the week, making input cost planning challenging for producers with significant fuel exposure.
sections.bottom_line: Energy weakness could provide cost relief if sustained.

the_more_you_know.title: Why 52-Week Range Position Matters More Than Price
the_more_you_know.body: When corn sits at 86% of its 52-week range versus natural gas at just 2%, it reveals relative value stories. High range positions often signal either strong fundamentals or overextended moves ready for correction. Low range positions can indicate either genuine weakness or opportunity. Smart producers track these percentages alongside absolute prices to gauge whether current levels offer value or risk.

Weather models for Corn Belt rainfall

daily_quote.text: The four most dangerous words in investing are: this time it's different.

source_summary: CBOT, CME, NYMEX closing prices; USDA reports; weather services
